seo.title: Building Self-hosted application directories with Docker...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Self-hosted application directories with Docker and Coolify.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to setting up a self-hosted environment using Docker, Traefik, and VPS infrastructure, focusing on security, automation, and compliance with European data sovereignty requirements.
prerequisites: VPS from Hetzner or Contabo with Ubuntu 22.04
Domain name with DNS management access

steps.title: Configure VPS firewall and OS hardening
steps.explanation: Set up UFW firewall rules to allow SSH, HTTP, HTTPS, and required application ports. Disable root login and update system packages.

code.content:
ufw allow OpenSSH
ufw allow http
ufw allow https
ufw enable
sed -i 's/#PermitRootLogin prohibit-password/PermitRootLogin no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

pitfalls: Forgetting to allow specific application ports

steps.title: Install Traefik with Let's Encrypt integration
steps.explanation: Deploy Traefik as a reverse proxy with automatic SSL certificate generation using Let's Encrypt. Configure domain routing.

code.content:
version: '3.8'
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v2.9
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - /etc/traefik:/etc/traefik
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    command:
      - --api.insecure=true
      - --providers.docker=true
      - --certificatesresolvers.le.acme.email=your@email.com
      - --certificatesresolvers.le.acme.storage=/etc/traefik/acme.json

steps.title: Deploy application with Docker Compose
steps.explanation: Create a Docker Compose file for a sample application (e.g., WordPress) with Traefik routing and environment variables for database credentials.

code.content:
version: '3.8'
services:
  wordpress:
    image: wordpress:latest
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
    environment:
      WORDPRESS_DB_HOST: db:3306
      WORDPRESS_DB_USER: user
      WORDPRESS_DB_PASSWORD: password
    labels:
      - "traefik.http.routers.wordpress.rule=Host(`app.example.com`)")

pitfalls: Exposing container ports without Traefik routing

steps.title: Set up automated container updates
steps.explanation: Use Watchtower to monitor and update containers. Configure it to restart containers after updates.

code.content:
version: '3.8'
services:
  watchtower:
    image: containrrr/watchtower
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    command: --watch-stopped --no-pull

pitfalls: Not testing updates in staging environments
Forgetting to restart services after updates

steps.title: Implement backup strategy with BorgBackup
steps.explanation: Configure BorgBackup to create incremental backups of critical data. Set up retention policies and remote storage.
pitfalls: Storing backups in same physical location as primary data

content.conclusion: This setup provides a secure, automated self-hosted infrastructure with core components for application deployment, security, and maintenance. Regularly review logs, update dependencies, and validate backups to ensure reliability.
